scenes.text: In this scene, we introduce the concept of JavaScript functions. Functions are reusable blocks of code that perform a specific task. They can be defined once and used multiple times throughout a program. This concept is crucial for writing efficient and maintainable code, allowing programmers to avoid repetition and update functionality with ease.

The second scene explores control flow in programming through the lens of state diagrams. State diagrams visually represent the different states of a process and how transitions occur between states based on certain conditions or events. Understanding state diagrams can help in designing systems with varied states and comprehending how a system should react under different scenarios.
scenes.image_prompt:
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Uninitialized
    Uninitialized --> Initialized
    Initialized --> Processing
    Processing --> Paused
    Paused --> Processing
    Processing --> Completed
    Completed --> [*]
